title: Seabed Splice Router: Minimum-SWAP Channel Routing on a Cable Mesh
strategies: reset-to-home router: route the first channel to its partner along a shortest cable path each splice, then undo all swaps to restore the home placement (trivial, ~0.1)
no-reset SABRE-naive: keep channels where routing leaves them and route the first channel toward its partner in the live mapping (greedy)
meet-in-the-middle with look-ahead: same swap cost per splice but choose the split point that steers the pair toward the next W splices' channels (strong)
full token-swapping / SABRE with reverse-traversal + weighted look-ahead over the whole schedule -- true minimum SWAP count is NP-hard and unknown (open ceiling, uncovered)
